question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 65.00% on 2026-03-28 for the Metaculus question "Will the Community beat Nathan Young in the Metaculus Cup Spring 2026?"?
summary: This meta-prediction asks whether the CP on "Will the Community beat Nathan Young in the Metaculus Cup Spring 2026?" will be strictly above 65% on March 28. The CP has declined consistently from 85% to exactly 65% over 5 weeks with no reversal. The threshold is strict inequality, so status quo (65.00%) resolves NO. Monte Carlo simulations using observed drift (-4pp/week) project the median CP around 57-61% by March 28. Even generous mixture models allowing for trend reversal scenarios produce only 20-36% probability. The strong, unbroken downward trend with the CP sitting exactly at the boundary strongly favors NO.

factors.description: Strong and consistent downward trend in CP: declined from 85% to 65% over 5 weeks with no reversal in any week. Weekly changes: 0, -3, -2, -9, -6. Average drift of -4pp/week.
Current CP is exactly at the threshold (65.00%). For YES, it needs to be strictly above 65%. Status quo resolves NO. Even staying flat resolves NO.
Recent acceleration in decline: last 3 weeks averaged -5.67pp/week vs -4pp/week overall. Suggests the decline may be intensifying, not slowing.
194 forecasters on the underlying question - large base means CP is relatively stable but hard to move dramatically in either direction. Random noise alone is unlikely to push above threshold.
Tournament has ~2 months remaining with many unresolved questions. New question resolutions could provide information that shifts forecaster sentiment either way, creating some upward possibility.

Monte Carlo simulations: Trend-based models give 0-4% probability; attenuated drift gives ~27%; mixture model gives 20-24%. Even with generous scenario weights favoring reversal, probability stays below 36%.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.23 (23% probability YES)**

Key reasoning:

- **Consistent downward trend**: The CP has fallen from 85% → 65% over 5 weeks with zero reversal weeks. Average decline is -4pp/week, recently accelerating to ~-5.7pp/week.
- **Threshold at exact current value**: The CP is at precisely 65.00%, and the question requires *strictly above* 65%. Status quo = NO.
- **Simulation results**: Pure trend models give 0-4% probability; mixture models accounting for possible trend reversal give 20-24%; sensitivity analysis caps at ~36% even with generous reversal assumptions.
- **Upside risk**: Tournament has ~2 months remaining, and question resolutions could shift sentiment. But there's no concrete evidence suggesting the trend will reverse imminently.

The 23% reflects that while the trend strongly favors continued decline (NO), there's meaningful uncertainty about whether the drift rate will moderate or reverse over the next 12 days.

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve based on the community prediction of the Metaculus question found [here](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/41508) (i.e. the 'target question'). If the community prediction of the target question on 2026-03-28 23:52:09 is higher than 65.00%, this question will resolve to 'Yes'. If it is lower or equal to 65.00%, this question will resolve to 'No'. If the target question has already resolved before this question opens, then this question will be annulled. If the target question resolves after this question opens, but before 2026-03-28 23:52:09, then this question will resolve to the same value as the target question.
